name: Steve Chabot
subject: Are We Losing Our Country?
body:
Are We Losing Our Country? No Images? [Click here](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-e-jiklqg-kujkulhluu-z/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jiklqg-kujkulhluu-r/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jiklqg-kujkulhluu-y/)
### [Are We Losing Our Country?](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jiklqg-kujkulhluu-j/)
President Trump once declared that a country without borders is not really a country anymore. And he was right. And that's just what seems to be rapidly happening to the greatest country this world has ever known.

Click the link below to read more. [Read this entry >>](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jiklqg-kujkulhluu-t/)
# Previous Blog Posts
[](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jiklqg-kujkulhluu-i/)
### [Did the Democrats Just Re-Elect President Trump at Their Debates Last Week?](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jiklqg-kujkulhluu-d/)
It is my humble opinion, that the Democrats, in their very first debate last week, went a considerable way towards re-electing President Trump in the upcoming 2020 election. I'm on the blog talking about it today.

Click the link below to read more. [Read this entry >>](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jiklqg-kujkulhluu-h/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jiklqg-kujkulhluu-k/)
### [Here Come the Democrat Debates - Aaaaah!](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jiklqg-kujkulhluu-u/)
Tonight and tomorrow night 20 Democrats are going to be debating each other. Each thinks he or she ought to be the next president of the United States. I'm on the blog talking about what we can expect.

Click the link below to read more. [Read this entry >>](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jiklqg-kujkulhluu-o/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jiklqg-kujkulhluu-b/)
### [Hidin' Joe Biden](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jiklqg-kujkulhluu-n/)
Joe Biden is clearly the frontrunner at this time to win the Democratic nomination for president. He's way ahead in virtually all the polls. But where exactly IS Joe Biden?

Click the link below to read more in this week's blog. body:

subject: Ivory Coast passes legislation encouraged by Ivanka Trump
body:
"Ivanka Trump is applauding the recent passage of legislation in Ivory Coast related to changes she pushed during her April trip to Africa." | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=6223529a98&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **Ivory Coast passes legislation encouraged by Ivanka Trump**

“Ivanka Trump is applauding the recent passage of legislation in Ivory Coast related to changes she pushed during her April trip to Africa,” Jill Colvin and Alexis Adele report for _The Associated Press_.

“The country is in the process of updating its family code to make it more equitable to women — a move President Donald Trump’s eldest daughter and senior adviser praised as ‘a great step forward.’” The new law will support women’s ownership rights and their equal management of household assets.

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=405e4fcfff&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “A White House initiative spearheaded by Ivanka Trump to help women in developing countries get ahead economically announced its first batch of grants on Wednesday: $27 million for 14 projects in 22 countries, mostly in Africa, Latin America and Southeast Asia,” Darlene Superville writes in [_The Associated Press_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=7cf3178f50&e=3ee4985eea). “The projects are meant to help women with employment and entrepreneurship and to provide women in business with access to financing and other assistance.”
---
|
---
| “Since last fall, the Department of Homeland Security has consistently stated that Border Patrol’s temporary holding spaces were not designed nor ever intended to detain the record numbers of migrants illegally crossing the border, including tens of thousands of unaccompanied children and families every month,” members of a bipartisan Homeland Security Advisory council write in [_USA Today_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=d0b4d2dbdf&e=3ee4985eea). “The Border Patrol did not cause this crisis; our broken immigration system did. To fix this problem requires immediate changes to our laws, not just funding.”
---
|
---
| “Ensuring farmers have long-term, free and fair access to our largest trade markets is critical to empowering the next generation of family farmers,” Iowa’s Secretary of Agriculture Mike Naig writes in [_The Hill_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=b72ada061b&e=3ee4985eea). “While the latest round of federal trade aid assistance will provide some much needed, short-term relief for farm families, farmers need and want markets to sell their products. Congress has the power to open additional market opportunities right now by ratifying the U.S.-Mexico-Canada Agreement (USMCA).”
---
|

[Privacy Policy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=7b18edce10&e=3ee4985eea) | [Contact the White House](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=33a9bd5701&e=3ee4985eea) | [Unsubscribe](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=a2e708d4dd)
---
| | | The White House * 1600 Pennsylvania Ave NW * Washington, DC 20500 * USA · 202-456-1111
---

subject: Urge Congress to pass paid leave for new moms and dads. Now.

| | The math doesn't add up. And new parents are paying the price. Nationwide, there's no federally guaranteed paid leave for new parents. For too many Americans, that means juggling obligations or struggling to pay bills _if_ they can't work -- all on too little sleep and with a newborn at home. In Oregon, a _bipartisan_ majority just passed a paid family leave law, one of just a handful of states that have done it already. name: Tim Kaine
subject: Acosta must go
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
| | Derek:

A decade ago, a U.S. Attorney named Alexander Acosta handed serial sexual predator Jeffrey Epstein a secret, sweetheart deal that gave him an incredibly lenient sentence for atrocious crimes. That attorney is now Secretary of Labor in President Trump's cabinet.

Acosta _never_ should have been confirmed as Labor Secretary. I asked him about this plea deal during his confirmation hearing, and his answer in no way reassured me. I voted against his confirmation. And now, amidst new criminal charges filed against Epstein, I'm calling for his resignation. [**Add your name to mine and say Acosta must go.**](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f177b/3e0906/7893aab3/3e3092fc/2977458067/VEsH/ "https://secure.timkaine.com/page/s/acosta-must-go?utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=1+-+Add+your+name+to+mine+and+say+Acosta+mus&utm_campaign=20190711_1_PET_TK_Acosta&source=20190711_1_PET_TK_Acosta")

Survivors of Epstein's abuse have been denied their day in court for far too long, thanks to Acosta's egregious dealings. Had the case gone to trial, Epstein could have received life in prison for his sex trafficking and sexual abuse of young girls. Instead, Acosta's deal allowed him to serve just 13 months -- during which he was allowed to _leave_ prison for 12 hours a day, six days a week, to go work at a nearby office. **And not only did Acosta cut this private deal for a monstrous predator, he also failed to disclose that deal to the survivors, robbing them of their right to decide whether or not they would testify against their abuser.**

Acosta gave a nearly hour-long press conference yesterday to try to defend the indefensible (during which he didn't _once_ offer any kind of apology to the survivors of Epstein's abuses). But here's what I still want to know:
* Acosta claims that he struck the deal to protect victims from tough cross-examination in a trial. But why didn't he let survivors decide for themselves whether to take the stand?
* And why did he work so hard to keep the deal hidden from them, violating their rights in the process?
* By letting Epstein plead to a prostitution charge, he insulted the survivors and failed to inform others that he was a predator. How does Acosta justify letting Epstein whitewash his crimes?

**The American public -- and more importantly, the survivors of Epstein's abuse -- deserve answers to these questions. Acosta must resign, and he must answer for his serious errors in judgment.**

[**Derek, sign my petition to demand Acosta 's resignation.**](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f177b/3e0906/7893aab3/3e3092f3/2977458067/VEsE/ "https://secure.timkaine.com/page/s/acosta-must-go?utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=2+-+FIRSTNAMEIf+you+agree+sign+my+petition+t&utm_campaign=20190711_1_PET_TK_Acosta&source=20190711_1_PET_TK_Acosta")

President Trump said in the Oval Office this week that he feels "very badly" for Acosta. I don't. I grieve for dozens of young girls who were trafficked by Jeffrey Epstein and denied the justice and transparency they are owed. And I will do what I can to hold Acosta accountable for his decision to deny them that justice.

Thank you for standing with me today.

-Tim Kaine

clean_body: | | Derek: A decade ago, a U.S. Attorney named Alexander Acosta handed serial sexual predator Jeffrey Epstein a secret, sweetheart deal that gave him an incredibly lenient sentence for atrocious crimes. That attorney is now Secretary of Labor in President Trump's cabinet. Acosta never should have been confirmed as Labor Secretary. I asked him about this plea deal during his confirmation hearing, and his answer in no way reassured me. I voted against his confirmation. And now, amidst new criminal charges filed against Epstein, I'm calling for his resignation. "" Survivors of Epstein's abuse have been denied their day in court for far too long, thanks to Acosta's egregious dealings. Had the case gone to trial, Epstein could have received life in prison for his sex trafficking and sexual abuse of young girls. Instead, Acosta's deal allowed him to serve just 13 months -- during which he was allowed to leave prison for 12 hours a day, six days a week, to go work at a nearby office. And not only did Acosta cut this private deal for a monstrous predator, he also failed to disclose that deal to the survivors, robbing them of their right to decide whether or not they would testify against their abuser. Acosta gave a nearly hour-long press conference yesterday to try to defend the indefensible (during which he didn't once offer any kind of apology to the survivors of Epstein's abuses). But here's what I still want to know: Acosta claims that he struck the deal to protect victims from tough cross-examination in a trial. But why didn't he let survivors decide for themselves whether to take the stand? And why did he work so hard to keep the deal hidden from them, violating their rights in the process? By letting Epstein plead to a prostitution charge, he insulted the survivors and failed to inform others that he was a predator. How does Acosta justify letting Epstein whitewash his crimes? The American public -- and more importantly, the survivors of Epstein's abuse -- deserve answers to these questions. Acosta must resign, and he must answer for his serious errors in judgment. "" President Trump said in the Oval Office this week that he feels "very badly" for Acosta. I don't. I grieve for dozens of young girls who were trafficked by Jeffrey Epstein and denied the justice and transparency they are owed. And I will do what I can to hold Acosta accountable for his decision to deny them that justice. Thank you for standing with me today. -Tim Kaine | ** ""** | "" "" ""

| | | Did you see this call to action from Debbie? We must step up to demand the Trump administration stop its cruel family separation and inhumane treatment of children.

**Add your name now:** [https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/Close-the-Detention-Centers](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz. "https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/Close-the-Detention-Centers?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Englewood&state=FL&zip=34223&phone=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=schultz&utm_content=1+-+httpssecuredebbiewassermanschultzcomClos&utm_campaign=DWS_EM_PT_90DO_190711_1_2&source=DWS_EM_PT_90DO_190711_1_2")

Begin Forwarded Message:
> Friend, we've seen the horrific news stories and reports coming out of the migrant detention facilities at the border.
>
> It's unacceptable that the Trump administration has denied detained children basic hygiene items like toothbrushes, soap, and towels -- for days on end. This cruel treatment must stop.
>
> Let's send a message to the Trump administration. [Add your name to stand with us and demand an end to family separation and the inhumane treatment of children.](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz. "https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/Close-the-Detention-Centers?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Englewood&state=FL&zip=34223&phone=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=schultz&utm_content=2+-+Add+your+name+to+stand+with+us+and+deman&utm_campaign=DWS_EM_PT_90DO_190711_1_2&source=DWS_EM_PT_90DO_190711_1_2")

> My colleagues and I have called for an investigation of the company that runs the Homestead detention center for migrant children here in Florida. We've also called for its closure after seeing of the center's heartbreaking, prison-like conditions that hundreds of children endure there every day -- and it's all happening while Congress is denied basic, unfettered oversight of this facility in violation of the law. But we can't stop there.
>
> We must fight back against the Trump administration's cruel and inhumane treatment of children at detention centers along the border. It's our duty to make our voices heard now.
>

> Debbie

| This week my team reached out asking for your feedback to our July Priorities Survey, and I wanted to take a minute to share some of the initial results with you.

These were the top issues from thousands of Democrats across the country:
[](http://ma.edmarkey. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/markey-july2019-weekly?refcode=EM-071119&amount=10&recurring=true&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=") **President Trump and Republicans are attacking these issues every single day.**

* Trump's budget proposed slashing BILLIONS from Social Security, Medicare, and Medicaid.

* Our climate-denier-in-chief continues to ignore the science about climate change and instead kowtows to Big Oil and King Coal at our planet's peril.

* And just this week, Obamacare was put in jeopardy again by a lawsuit aiming to destroy the entire health care system. If Trump and the GOP get their way, hundreds of thousands of people in Massachusetts and millions across the country will lose their coverage.

I am fighting back every day to hold President Trump accountable and stop his administration's hateful agenda. But I can't be in this fight alone. I need your support to fight for our values.

**Right now my campaign is coming up short of this week 's fundraising goal, and with national Republicans targeting our race to turn Massachusetts red in 2020, we can't afford to miss a single goal.**

Trump's budget proposed slashing BILLIONS from Social Security, Medicare, and Medicaid. Our climate-denier-in-chief continues to ignore the science about climate change and instead kowtows to Big Oil and King Coal at our planet's peril. And just this week, Obamacare was put in jeopardy again by a lawsuit aiming to destroy the entire health care system. If Trump and the GOP get their way, hundreds of thousands of people in Massachusetts and millions across the country will lose their coverage. I am fighting back every day to hold President Trump accountable and stop his administration's hateful agenda. But I can't be in this fight alone. I need your support to fight for our values. Right now my campaign is coming up short of this week 's fundraising goal, and with national Republicans targeting our race to turn Massachusetts red in 2020, we can't afford to miss a single goal. name: Dick Durbin
subject: Amy McGrath isn't afraid of Mitch McConnell
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Dick Durbin
body:
#
[**Amy McGrath is taking on Mitch McConnell in Kentucky. Contribute $5 now.**](http://action.dickdurbin. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dd-mcgrath?refcode=em190711&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=34223&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&addr1=&addr2=&city=Englewood&state=FL&phone=&employer=&occupation=")
Peter,
When Amy McGrath was just 13 years old growing up in Kentucky, she dreamed of one day becoming a fighter pilot. So she wrote a letter to her Senator, Mitch McConnell, and asked him to change the laws that prohibited women from serving in combat. She never got a reply.
Amy persevered and achieved her dreams anyway, overcoming the odds to become the first woman in the Marine Corps to fly a combat mission in an F/A-18 fighter jet. Now she's taking on another important fight--she's challenging Mitch McConnell and running for Senate in 2020.
[**I 'm all in for Amy because she has what it takes to defeat Mitch McConnell and help us take back the Senate. Will you join me in supporting her with a $5 contribution now?**](http://action.dickdurbin. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dd-mcgrath?refcode=em190711&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=34223&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&addr1=&addr2=&city=Englewood&state=FL&phone=&employer=&occupation=")
As Majority Leader, Mitch McConnell's tenure has been disastrous for our country. He has blocked urgent legislation on everything from reforming our gun laws to protecting Dreamers, all while fast-tracking the confirmation of radically conservative judges.
We need to elect a Democratic majority in 2020--but we also need to send a clear message to politicians like Mitch McConnell that the people have had enough of his divisive agenda. The best way to do that is at the ballot box. That's why we all need to have Amy's back in this fight.
[**Let 's help Amy defeat Mitch McConnell. Help launch her campaign--chip in $5 now.**](http://action.dickdurbin. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dd-mcgrath?refcode=em190711&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=34223&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&addr1=&addr2=&city=Englewood&state=FL&phone=&employer=&occupation=")
My best,
Dick Durbin

name: Elizabeth Warren
subject: Our plan to reform our immigration system
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren for President
body:
We know that immigrants helped weave the very fabric of our country in the past -- and we know that immigrants belong here today. We need big, structural change to fix our immigration system. | | | [](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852ee17/45c30650/175883ff4/669cd70e/3458676567/VEsH/ "https://elizabethwarren.com/")
---
Today, I'm announcing my plan for immigration reform.

Immigrants are our neighbors, our friends, our family -- they make our country stronger and more diverse.

But our immigration system is broken, and we need real reform. We can create a rules-based system that is fair, humane, and that reflects our values, from expanding legal immigration and creating a fair and achievable path to citizenship to ending the hatred, ugliness, and chaos at the border.

**[If you want an immigration system that preserves our security, grows our economy, and reflects our values, then be a part of the grassroots movement that will make it happen. Add your name if you 're with me.](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852ee17/45c30650/175883ff4/669cd771/3458676567/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiIyMDE5MDcxMWVtMS1mdWxsLWEifQ==/ "https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/s/ew-immigration-reform?source=20190711em1-full-a&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")**

When I say that our immigration system needs to reflect our values, here's what I plan to do:

**1\. Eliminate abusive enforcement and significantly reduce immigrant detention.**

Entering the country without authorization has always been a violation of civil immigration law, but thanks to a former segregationist Senator, it's also a criminal violation. This criminal prohibition is totally unnecessary for border security, and we should repeal it. As president, I will immediately end criminal prosecutions for simple administrative immigration violations and refocus our limited resources on actual criminals and real threats to the United States.

I will also reshape CBP and ICE from top to bottom. Our immigration agencies should protect Americans and uphold the rule of law, not pursue punitive policies that target communities of color. I'll hold immigration enforcement to the same due process standards as other law enforcement agencies and strengthen watchdogs to prevent future abuses.

Under a Warren administration, if anyone's violated the basic rights of immigrants, we'll hold them accountable. That includes the abuses committed under the Trump administration. I'll set up a DOJ task force to investigate accusations of serious violations -- including medical neglect and physical and sexual assaults -- and give it independent authority to pursue any criminal allegations.

And I'll end unnecessary detention and eliminate private detention facilities. We already have the tools to effectively track and monitor individuals without shoving them into cages and camps. As president, I'll issue guidance ensuring detention is only used when an individual poses a flight or safety risk.

**2\. Provide rights and due process in our immigration courts.**

To prevent future abuses, a Warren administration will establish professional, independent immigration courts and recruit highly qualified immigration judges with a diverse set of legal experiences so that everyone receives appropriate justice.

Right now, most immigrants facing deportation do not have attorneys -- and in the Trump administration, that even includes toddlers. I'll eliminate the use of expedited removal proceedings, guarantee hearings, and provide access to counsel in immigration court.

**3\. Welcome those in need.**

Our laws and our values compel us to help those fleeing violence and oppression. My administration will raise the refugee cap and reject exclusionary policies based on race, religion, and nationality. On my first day in office, I'll reverse Trump's bigoted Muslim Ban.

As president, I will also reverse Trump's efforts to stack the deck against asylum applicants. I'll ensure that asylum seekers can safely present themselves, restore President Obama's promise to extend asylum for those fleeing domestic or gang violence, and affirm asylum protections for gender identity and sexual orientation-based claims.

**4\. Grow legal immigration and establish a fair and achievable path to status.**

I'll work to expand legal immigration to grow our economy and reunify families. We should also make it easier for those eligible to naturalize, and repeal the 3- and 10-year bars preventing people from applying for legal status.

I'll take executive action to restore and expand protections for our Dreamers. And I'll extend deferred action protections to those who have contributed to our country for years and have built careers and families here, while also pursuing a legislative solution that provides a fair and achievable path to citizenship.

**5\. Address the root causes of migration.**

We cannot fully address migration until we address its root causes -- poverty, climate change, violence, and injustice. The solution to Central American migration isn't placing children in cages -- it's stabilizing the countries that families are risking their lives to escape.

As president, I'll restore and increase aid, step up efforts to address transnational crime, and protect those seeking refuge here.

Donald Trump wants to divide us -- to pit worker against worker, neighbor against neighbor. He wants Americans to blame their troubles on those who are new to our country, or who don't look the same, even as his administration robs us dry.

**But we know that immigrants helped weave the very fabric of our country in the past -- and we know that immigrants belong here today.

[We need big, structural change to fix our immigration system. And we need a grassroots movement to make it happen. Add your name today, and let's fix this broken system together.](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852ee17/45c30650/175883ff4/669cd771/3458676567/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiIyMDE5MDcxMWVtMS1mdWxsLWEifQ==/ "https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/s/ew-immigration-reform?source=20190711em1-full-a&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")**

Thanks for being a part of this,

Elizabeth

subject: Can Kamala count on you to represent in Englewood?
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY KAMALA HARRIS FOR THE PEOPLE
body:
|
#
Kamala has been criss-crossing America delivering her positive message and vision for our country to as many people as possible at town halls, house parties, and community meetings. But she can’t be everywhere. **That’s why the debates are so important: it’s a moment where she can reach millions of Americans.** There’s another Democratic debate at the end of this month, and we need to start organizing now to make it even bigger and better than the last one. **[Can we count on you to host a debate watch party in your area?](https://action.kamalaharris.org/sign/org-july-debate-commit?refcode=ema190710-debatehost2-full&source=ema190710-debatehost2-full&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Englewood&state=FL&zip=34223&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&t=1&akid=5560%2E5755789%2Ek_nugR)** | | [COMMIT TO HOST](https://action.kamalaharris.org/sign/org-july-debate-commit?refcode=ema190710-debatehost2-full&source=ema190710-debatehost2-full&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Englewood&state=FL&zip=34223&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&t=2&akid=5560%2E5755789%2Ek_nugR)

We won’t know which night Kamala will take the stage -- either July 30 or July 31 -- until the lineup is announced on July 18. But that won’t stop us from organizing now! The more people get to know Kamala and hear directly from her, the stronger our movement grows.
**[We’re counting on you to make sure as many people as possible are watching Kamala in this next debate. Commit to hosting a watch party in your area:](https://action.kamalaharris.org/sign/org-july-debate-commit?refcode=ema190710-debatehost2-full&source=ema190710-debatehost2-full&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Englewood&state=FL&zip=34223&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&t=3&akid=5560%2E5755789%2Ek_nugR)**

| Friend, I'm M.E., and I've joined the team to help take this campaign to the next level--specifically, to accelerate our momentum going into the next round of debates.

All campaigns have growth stages, and we're in a new one--a critical one. I'm writing today both to introduce myself and to ask you to keep one thing in mind:

**No matter what you hear or read in the news --this race is _wide open_.**

It's true there's no shortage of opinions, from pundits to Politico. Opinions and pot-stirring are what they're paid to provide. But the fact remains: _There are seven months left until the first votes are cast in Iowa, and 60% of Democratic primary voters haven't really picked who they're voting for._

What John offers is unique in this race. He's the only candidate who's run a small business, a city, and state, bringing people together across the aisle to achieve progressive results in a way no other candidate has. **In fact, he 's already delivered the big, progressive victories that Washington has failed to make good on--and that other candidates are just talking about.**

| Team, we sent you an important petition yesterday demanding that Congress strengthen parental leave laws.

_But we noticed that your signature is missing._

This is a small, but critical, action we can all take to help make real change on this crucial issue.

[Take a moment to read yesterday's note and add your name:](https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/m/35cbadba/134673f2/124164/69968a62/1428164498/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZGYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVkdhWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "https://www.signherenow.org/petition/paid-family-leave/bennet?source=EM_PET_B2_071119_AC_ND&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=")

---
| [ADD YOUR NAME ->](https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/m/35cbadba/134673f2/124164/69968a62/1428164498/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZGYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVkdhWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "https://www.signherenow.org/petition/paid-family-leave/bennet?source=EM_PET_B2_071119_AC_ND&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=")
---
* * *
Team, we need your help. It will only take two minutes and it costs you absolutely nothing -- but it could help make a huge impact.

We know that the period after a child is born is critical for development.

Unfortunately, the United States is failing when it comes to family and medical leave laws.

We are gathering 2,000 signatures to demand that every member of Congress stands up for change.

[TELL CONGRESS: STRENGTHEN OUR PARENTAL LEAVE LAWS!](https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/m/35cbadba/134673f2/124164/69968a62/1428164498/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZGYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVkdhWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "https://www.signherenow.org/petition/paid-family-leave/bennet?source=EM_PET_B2_071119_AC_ND&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=")
| | [ADD YOUR NAME ->](https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/m/35cbadba/134673f2/124164/69968a62/1428164498/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZGYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVkdhWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "https://www.signherenow.org/petition/paid-family-leave/bennet?source=EM_PET_B2_071119_AC_ND&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=")
---

Across the world, in the vast majority of developed countries, paid leave is the standard.

In the United States? Parents are only guaranteed 12 weeks of _unpaid_ leave and that's only if the company employs over 50 people.

More than half of American businesses don't have to guarantee any leave for parents.

This is a shameful practice for the most prosperous country on Earth, and it's up to all of us to demand change.

[Let's send a message: pass real parental leave policies now!](https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/m/35cbadba/134673f2/124164/69968a62/1428164498/VEsD/p/eyJKU1ZGYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVkdhWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "https://www.signherenow.org/petition/paid-family-leave/bennet?source=EM_PET_B2_071119_AC_ND&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=")

subject: HUGE fundraising numbers
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Eric Holcomb for Indiana
body:
Because of support from Hoosiers like you this fundraising period, Governor Holcomb now has $6.03 million cash-on-hand, which is more than any... | | [](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=787e533357ee424a93cbf264ffd9a69d&_e=PtlzgHFuk1f6oKto5DcjEi9WG5okNWIRmtqTy4B6foPe1KxI9J3MkHhzJ8IxECo0_Aj58SicCWrgSvIJYxWrUCQMXK_WeF4wC9-t19WvbVVh5EwpsDbudQNl8fx6qrzlNu87ZsO0J9QLQFGgHBTLsLjsemQ4JZksa5_U8xbtGeAoN08UjllkiSBbjdjehR-GUJIoBieg4gRTpqtmsd8rh46FuGaHk4qeztmpw_u8fb_k6UVRAN8KkfzDJf0YYdob52zIZW7R52xb_QDcpEwhd4YzXkEuQJin45X9lVLRavy8n4Jc13_frl-K3FjnHUDhuXcYJF1oOREY73GiflZkCfqd72hfoJ_oJEtO2OkbRjoe_VrxNvjRA7dAOW8LE_QHTrjIEhEsjphDpHb1bnyPNxvzQcYEi55T3G89QPdPvio%3D) |
---|---|---
Friend,

**Governor Holcomb’s first fundraising period of 2019 was a success!** We saw an historic outpouring of support of support from Hoosiers, and Governor Holcomb is so grateful.

**Because of support from Hoosiers like you this fundraising period, Governor Holcomb now has $6.03 million cash-on-hand,** which is more than any previous Indiana governor's cash-on-hand at the midpoint of their third year in office.

**Then, when you combine his fundraising with the Indiana Republican Party and Lt. Governor Suzanne Crouch, there’s another record -- $7.23 million in the bank! **

It's safe to say Hoosiers are seeing a trend from Governor Eric Holcomb. After posting record fundraising numbers and earning a sky-high approval rating at the end of 2018, Governor Holcomb is at it again at the end of another fundraising period. Hoosiers have placed Governor Holcomb in a historic position because of his people-focused approach to leading our state and because of the unprecedented results that approach is delivering across the state.

_Governor Holcomb relies on grassroots donations from Hoosiers like you to help keep Indiana’s momentum surging and to make this record-breaking fundraising possible. Without you, Governor Holcomb cannot continue to achieve historic accomplishments for Hoosiers._

_What are those accomplishments?_

We’ve seen record job commitments, record infrastructure investments, new workforce development opportunities and a state government that is becoming leaner and more responsive to the needs of Hoosiers under Governor Holcomb’s leadership, but we’re just getting started.

YOU are making this possible – and we want to keep this momentum going.

clean_body: Because of support from Hoosiers like you this fundraising period, Governor Holcomb now has $6.03 million cash-on-hand, which is more than any... | | | ---|---|--- Friend, Governor Holcomb’s first fundraising period of 2019 was a success! We saw an historic outpouring of support of support from Hoosiers, and Governor Holcomb is so grateful. Because of support from Hoosiers like you this fundraising period, Governor Holcomb now has $6.03 million cash-on-hand, which is more than any previous Indiana governor's cash-on-hand at the midpoint of their third year in office. Then, when you combine his fundraising with the Indiana Republican Party and Lt. Governor Suzanne Crouch, there’s another record -- $7.23 million in the bank! It's safe to say Hoosiers are seeing a trend from Governor Eric Holcomb. After posting record fundraising numbers and earning a sky-high approval rating at the end of 2018, Governor Holcomb is at it again at the end of another fundraising period. Hoosiers have placed Governor Holcomb in a historic position because of his people-focused approach to leading our state and because of the unprecedented results that approach is delivering across the state. Governor Holcomb relies on grassroots donations from Hoosiers like you to help keep Indiana’s momentum surging and to make this record-breaking fundraising possible. Without you, Governor Holcomb cannot continue to achieve historic accomplishments for Hoosiers. What are those accomplishments? We’ve seen record job commitments, record infrastructure investments, new workforce development opportunities and a state government that is becoming leaner and more responsive to the needs of Hoosiers under Governor Holcomb’s leadership, but we’re just getting started.
